summary: DHH shares a research study finding that regular swearing at work can boost team spirit and morale. He connects this to his well-known comfort with profanity in professional settings. The post highlights researchers' conclusions that swearing helps employees express feelings and build social relationships. It's a brief endorsement of the idea that workplace culture should be open to unconventional norms.
key_points: Research supports the idea that swearing at work boosts team spirit and morale
Profanity can help employees express feelings more authentically and build social bonds
Leaders should be open to unconventional ideas about workplace culture and communication norms
money_quotes: Regular swearing at work can help boost team spirit among staff, allowing them to express better their feelings as well as develop social relationships
key_insight: Authentic communication at work, including swearing, can strengthen team bonds and morale rather than undermining professionalism.
